title: Stop asking AI to write. Here's what the NowThis audience strategist does instead.
tldr: NowThis's head of audience argues communicators should treat AI as a team of specialists (research, analysis, distribution) rather than a writer. A fresh reframe of AI's role in content workflows.
why_fresh: reframes AI beyond writing into role-based workflows
angle: Write about building an 'AI team of specialists' workflow instead of using AI as a copy generator.

title: Why B2B Marketers Should Pay Attention to Microdramas
tldr: CMI spotlights the microdrama format — short, episodic serialized video — as an emerging B2B storytelling vehicle. A genuinely new format trend worth tracking.
why_fresh: new episodic short-form format entering B2B
angle: Explore whether microdramas are the next serialized content pillar for brand storytelling.

title: AI Crawls vs. AI Traffic: What 560,000+ Requests Reveal About Where AI Actually Sends Visitors
tldr: Orbit Media analyzes 560k+ requests to separate AI crawling from actual referral traffic, showing where AI engines really send visitors. Concrete data on the shifting discovery landscape.
why_fresh: original data on AI referral vs crawl behavior
angle: Unpack what the crawl-vs-traffic gap means for content strategy in an answer-engine world.

title: The Scoop: Tinder's rebrand goes deeper than just adopting a new voice
tldr: Tinder's first rebrand in a decade includes a 'daring and witty' new voice, illustrating how tone and narrative shifts signal deeper repositioning. A useful brand-voice case study.
angle: Break down how a voice change is really a corporate narrative reset, not cosmetics.

title: Want to build audience and revenue through live events? These five news nonprofits have some ideas
tldr: Five nonprofit newsrooms share concrete playbooks for turning live events into audience and revenue engines — a standing-room-only topic at INN Days. Practical owned-media diversification.
why_fresh: live events as revenue trend with concrete examples
angle: Argue that live events are the underrated pillar of owned-media and community strategy.

title: The professionals behind that sponcon on your feed
tldr: Marketplace profiles the growing behind-the-scenes labor force powering influencer sponsored content — the invisible professionalization of the creator economy.
angle: Write on how the creator economy is becoming a full production industry, not solo hustle.

title: Travel's Creator Economy Doesn't Need a Million Followers. It Needs Proof It Converts.
tldr: Skift argues travel brands are shifting from follower counts to conversion proof when working with creators — a maturation signal for influencer marketing measurement.
why_fresh: shift from reach to conversion in creator deals
angle: Make the case that micro-creator conversion beats vanity reach for brand content.

title: Личный опыт молодого специалиста: как расти в карьере с помощью ИИ
tldr: A young professional details moving beyond basic AI generation to using it as a trainer for managerial skills and cutting task time tenfold. Practical, human-centered take on AI upskilling.
why_fresh: AI as a skills trainer, not just generator
angle: Explore using AI as a coach/simulator for building soft and management skills.

title: The AI skill no one is talking about
tldr: PR Daily argues the crucial AI competency isn't prompting or tech but judgment — knowing when and how to apply AI in comms work. A useful corrective to prompt-obsession.
why_fresh: argues judgment beats prompting as the key AI skill
angle: Write about editorial judgment as the real differentiating AI skill for communicators.

title: HubSpot Spent 20 Years Selling Trust. It Took Four Days of Fine Print to Put It on the Line.
tldr: A breakdown of how HubSpot's fine-print misstep threatened two decades of trust equity — a sharp corporate reputation and crisis-comms case study.
angle: Analyze how small governance lapses can undo years of trust-based brand equity.

title: The Red Chickz TikTok strategy powers hot chicken growth
tldr: A concrete campaign breakdown of how a hot-chicken chain used a TikTok-first strategy to fuel expansion. Tangible short-form video case study.
why_fresh: routine brand TikTok case, but concrete
angle: Use it as a template for how SMB brands can build organic short-form growth loops.

title: Illustrator pricing in 2026: what 403 artists told us about day rates, licensing & the 20% who feel fairly paid
tldr: A survey of 403 illustrators reveals surprisingly low median day rates and widespread feelings of underpayment. A data-driven pulse on the creative economy amid AI pressure.
why_fresh: original 2026 survey data on creative pay
angle: Explore what illustrator pay pressure signals about AI's squeeze on creative labor.

title: ABC will trial using AI for journalism. What are the risks and benefits?
tldr: Australia's ABC reverses its cautious AI stance via an Anthropic deal, letting staff use Claude in news production. A notable shift in newsroom AI governance.
angle: Discuss how AI governance policies evolve once caution meets competitive pressure.

title: 20 ways social media algorithms affect how you feel
tldr: Quartz surveys the psychological effects of social algorithms on mood and behavior — relevant to how audiences actually consume content.
why_fresh: aggregation of known effects, not new
angle: Tie algorithmic mood effects to how brands should design for emotional context.

title: News sources in Australia 2026, by age
tldr: Statista data breaks down where different age cohorts get their news in 2026 — direct evidence of shifting media-consumption habits.
angle: Map how generational news habits should reshape omnichannel content planning.
